prestazioni: Replace the original Type 547/1 1.6L flat-four (engine code: 547/1) with a fully rebuilt and balanced version featuring forged Mahle 90mm pistons (compression ratio increased to 10.5:1), titanium intake valves from Schrick, and a custom camshaft profile by Kelford Cams (280° duration, 12mm lift) for optimized high-RPM breathing while retaining the original dual-overhead-camshaft (DOHC) and dry-sump lubrication system.

Install a bespoke fuel system with dual Weber 46 IDM carburetors (replacing the original Solex units) paired with a custom intake manifold by ITG Maxogen, featuring velocity stacks and a ram-air induction setup via a hidden duct in the front valence. Fuel delivery is managed by an Aeromotive A1000 fuel pump and -6AN braided lines with AN-8 feed/return for consistent flow under sustained high-RPM use.

Upgrade the drivetrain with a close-ratio 5-speed gearbox (Type 741) from a 356 Carrera 2, fitted with a limited-slip differential by Quaife ATB (25% preload) and a lightweight aluminum flywheel by Sachs Performance. The clutch is a single-plate ceramic unit by Tilton Engineering (7.25" diameter) with a hydraulic throwout bearing for precise engagement. The driveshaft is replaced with a carbon-fiber unit by GKN Driveline to reduce rotational mass.
estetica: Apply a full custom body restoration using 0.8mm aluminum panels (replacing the original steel) by RML Group, with hand-beaten fender flares in the style of the 356 Carrera GTL Abarth, widening the track by 30mm. The front bumper is replaced with a lightweight aluminum unit from a 356 Carrera GT (1960), while the rear features a custom ducktail spoiler in carbon fiber by RWB Japan, adjustable for downforce tuning.

Install a period-correct but upgraded interior with Recaro SRD bucket seats (retaining the original 356 Carrera GT’s aluminum seat frames) upholstered in black Alcantara with red stitching by Saddleback Leather. The dashboard is refinished in matte black with a custom gauge pod by Stack Instruments (featuring a 10,000 RPM tachometer, oil temperature, and fuel pressure gauges), while the steering wheel is a 350mm suede-wrapped Momo Prototipo with a quick-release hub.

Fit a custom wide-body aero kit by TechArt (modified for the 356 B), including carbon fiber side skirts, a front splitter with integrated brake cooling ducts, and a rear diffuser with titanium mounting hardware. The wheels are 15x7" and 15x8" magnesium center-lock units by Fuchs (original 356 Carrera GT spec) wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (185/65 R15 front, 225/60 R15 rear) for optimal grip. The entire body is finished in Porsche GT Silver Metallic with a clear bra by XPEL applied to high-impact areas.
